rulings.Case Title: RALPH WARNER et al VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Realign Defendant-Heir As Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 14, 2016 in Department 503, Line 1. Plaintiffs' and Petitioner's motion to realign defendant-heir Betty Warner as plaintiff is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL RHODES VS. ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 14, 2016 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Puget Sound Commerce Center, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that Privette v. Superior Court (1993) 5 Cal.4th 689, and its progeny, bar plaintiff's claims against it. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and special interrogatory responses preclude defendant from sustaining its initial burden because defendant's alleged liability is based on the affirmative conduct of defendant's employees, not on the theories discussed in Privette and its progeny. Even if plaintiff cannot demonstrate that defendant failed to warn of a concealed, pre-existing hazardous condition on the premises or negligently exercised control over plaintiff's work, defendant has not shown that plaintiff cannot successfully assert his premises owner/contractor liability cause of action against it. As to causation, the substantial factor issue was the sole causation issue raised in defendant's Notice of Motion. Defendant failed to put forth undisputed material facts in its Separate Statement to properly raise this issue. Even if defendant properly raised this issue, it failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant was, in reasonable medical probability, a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his risk of developing his asbestos-related disease(s). Defendant did not demonstrate that plaintiff failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive written discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support such a claim and this failure precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. As to any issue related to threshold exposure - whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant - such issue was not properly raised by defendant. Any brief statements defendant made on this topic were not included in defendant's Notice of Motion or Separate Statement and only pertained to its argument related to Privette. Assuming that such issue was properly raised, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Although defendant titled its motion as a motion for summary judgment and, in the alternative, summary adjudication of issues, the court treated it only as a motion for summary judgment, based on the arguments made by defendant, defendant's Separate Statement, and CRC 3.1350. (Tentative ruling Continued, see next entry)

rulings.Case Title: MARY BUDDLE et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Bifurcate Trial On The Issue Of Medical Causation
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 14, 2016 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant BorgWarner Morse Tec LLC's motion to bifurcate trial on the issue of medical causation is denied without prejudice to being raised before the trial judge. As to plaintiffs' argument that the motion is untimely, the Court initially agreed with plaintiffs' argument that the motion is untimely, but after briefing on this motion was complete, on January 12, 2016, this Court approved the parties' stipulation and order to continue the trial date and to reopen limited expert discovery. As the trial date is now April 18, 2016, the motion is timely pursuant to C.C.P. § 598. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES SWAN VS. INGERSOLL-RAND COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 14, 2016 in Department 503, Line 4. Nancy Williams, Brayton Purcell LLP's motion to be relieved as counsel for plaintiff James Swan is denied without prejudice to filing another motion upon proper demonstration that the service requirements of CRC 3.1362 were satisfied. Brayton Purcell did not demonstrate that Mr. Swan received proper notice of this motion. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case.
